title: Signal Transduction Pathway Overview
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: college placement Biology
description: Interactive diagram exploring the three stages of cell signaling — reception, transduction, and response — through a GPCR-cAMP pathway with 13 labeled structures.

learningObjectives: Identify the three stages of cell signaling: reception, transduction, and response
Describe the role of GPCRs, G-proteins, and adenylyl cyclase in signal transduction
Explain how signal amplification occurs through second messengers and phosphorylation cascades
Distinguish between fast responses (enzyme activation) and slow responses (gene expression)

keyQuestions: How does a signal molecule outside the cell produce a response inside the cell without entering?
Where does signal amplification occur in the pathway?
Why can the same ligand produce different responses in different cell types?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think the signal molecule enters the cell — it does not for GPCR pathways
Students confuse the first messenger (ligand) with the second messenger (cAMP)
Students may not realize that signal amplification occurs at multiple steps
assessmentOpportunities: Quiz mode tests identification of all 13 pathway components
Ask students to trace the amplification at each step
Compare GPCR signaling to receptor tyrosine kinase signaling
